mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and rising recession probability, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, forecasting negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above two percent annualized during high inflation periods demonstrates supply-side resilience, supporting corporate margins and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent coincides with negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real interest rates, calculated as the federal funds rate minus monthly CPI growth, compress growth equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive real rates fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over a five-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings, generating positive quarterly equity returns through fundamental valuation channels.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns over rolling three-year windows with a Rank IC below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below one percent annualized signals economic stagnation and weak earnings momentum, triggering immediate negative market repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of quarters where GDP growth drops below one percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and retail sales, where production rises while sales fall, indicates inventory buildup and demand weakness, forecasting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO and RSAFS divergence shows no predictive power for 20-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 over out-of-sample periods.
